summary: This report presents CharacterFlywheel, an iterative flywheel process for improving large language models (LLMs) in production social chat applications across Instagram, WhatsApp, and Messenger. Starting from LLaMA 3.1, we refined models across 15 generations using data from both internal and external real-user traffic. Through continuous deployments from July 2024 to April 2025, we conducted controlled 7-day A/B tests showing consistent engagement improvements: 7 of 8 newly deployed models demonstrated positive lift over the baseline, with the strongest performers achieving up to 8.8% improvement in engagement breadth and 19.4% in engagement depth. We also observed substantial gains in steerability, with instruction following increasing from 59.2% to 84.8% and instruction violations decreasing from 26.6% to 5.8%. We detail the CharacterFlywheel process which integrates data curation, reward modeling to estimate and interpolate the landscape of engagement metrics, supervised fine-tuning (SFT), reinforcement learning (RL), and both offline and online evaluation to ensure reliable progress at each optimization step. We also discuss our methods for overfitting prevention and navigating production dynamics at scale. These contributions advance the scientific rigor and understanding of LLMs in social applications serving millions of users.

AI.tldr: CharacterFlywheel是一个迭代的飞轮过程，用于改进生产环境中的社交聊天应用LLM，通过15代模型迭代，在Instagram、WhatsApp和Messenger上实现了显著的参与度和可控性提升。
AI.motivation: 在Meta的社交聊天应用中改进LLM性能，解决生产环境中模型部署的挑战，提升用户参与度和模型可控性，同时确保科学严谨性和规模化应用。
AI.method: 从LLaMA 3.1开始，通过15代迭代优化，整合数据筛选、奖励建模（估计和插值参与度指标）、监督微调、强化学习，以及离线和在线评估，确保每个优化步骤的可靠进展。
AI.result: 在2024年7月至2025年4月的7天A/B测试中，8个新部署模型中有7个表现优于基线，最佳模型实现参与广度提升8.8%，参与深度提升19.4%；指令遵循从59.2%提高到84.8%，指令违规从26.6%降至5.8%。
AI.conclusion: CharacterFlywheel过程成功提升了社交应用中LLM的性能，展示了迭代优化方法在规模化生产环境中的有效性，为服务数百万用户的社交应用LLM提供了科学严谨的改进框架。

title: PonderLM-3: Adaptive Token-Wise Pondering with Differentiable Masking
summary: Test-time scaling has shown that allocating more additional computation at inference can improve generation quality, motivating a natural follow-up question: where should this computation be spent? Building on this insight, we introduce PonderLM-3, a pretraining framework for token-wise adaptive pondering that learns to selectively allocate additional computation under purely self-supervised objectives, built on top of the PonderLM-2 backbone. This makes additional inference computation an allocatable per-token resource, so tokens receive more computation only when it is beneficial, rather than paying a uniform extra cost. To make this allocation learnable while maintaining train-inference consistency, PonderLM-3 injects a differentiable attention mask during pretraining and pairs it with a matching hard pruning rule at inference. PonderLM-3 defines a stronger Pareto frontier: compared with existing recursive or adaptive baselines, it achieves lower pretraining perplexity at equal inference FLOPs. On downstream benchmarks, PonderLM-3 attains comparable performance to fixed-step PonderLM-2 under the same maximum number of additional computation steps, while using fewer inference FLOPs in practice. Overall, PonderLM-3 provides an end-to-end differentiable and train-inference consistent framework for token-wise adaptive computation, enabling additional inference compute to be allocated where it is most useful rather than paid uniformly by every token.

AI.tldr: PonderLM-3是一个预训练框架，通过token级别的自适应计算分配，在推理时根据需求为不同token分配不同计算量，而非统一增加计算成本。
AI.motivation: 测试时扩展显示增加推理计算可以提升生成质量，但关键问题是如何分配这些额外计算。现有方法通常对所有token统一增加计算成本，而PonderLM-3旨在让计算成为可按token分配的资源，只在有益时才增加计算。
AI.method: 在PonderLM-2基础上构建，通过注入可微注意力掩码进行预训练，配合推理时的硬剪枝规则，实现训练-推理一致性。框架学习在纯自监督目标下选择性分配额外计算。
AI.result: 在相同推理FLOPs下获得更低的预训练困惑度；在下游基准测试中，在相同最大额外计算步数下达到与固定步数PonderLM-2相当的性能，同时实际使用更少推理FLOPs。
AI.conclusion: PonderLM-3提供了一个端到端可微且训练-推理一致的token级别自适应计算框架，使额外推理计算能够分配到最需要的地方，而非由每个token统一承担成本。

title: SphUnc: Hyperspherical Uncertainty Decomposition and Causal Identification via Information Geometry
summary: Reliable decision-making in complex multi-agent systems requires calibrated predictions and interpretable uncertainty. We introduce SphUnc, a unified framework combining hyperspherical representation learning with structural causal modeling. The model maps features to unit hypersphere latents using von Mises-Fisher distributions, decomposing uncertainty into epistemic and aleatoric components through information-geometric fusion. A structural causal model on spherical latents enables directed influence identification and interventional reasoning via sample-based simulation. Empirical evaluations on social and affective benchmarks demonstrate improved accuracy, better calibration, and interpretable causal signals, establishing a geometric-causal foundation for uncertainty-aware reasoning in multi-agent settings with higher-order interactions.

AI.tldr: SphUnc：结合超球面表示学习与结构因果建模的统一框架，用于多智能体系统中的不确定性感知推理
AI.motivation: 复杂多智能体系统中的可靠决策需要校准的预测和可解释的不确定性。现有方法在处理高阶交互和不确定性分解方面存在不足。
AI.method: 使用von Mises-Fisher分布将特征映射到单位超球面潜在空间，通过信息几何融合将不确定性分解为认知和偶然成分，并在球面潜在变量上构建结构因果模型，支持基于样本的干预推理。
AI.result: 在社交和情感基准测试中表现出更高的准确性、更好的校准性，并产生可解释的因果信号。
AI.conclusion: SphUnc为具有高阶交互的多智能体环境中的不确定性感知推理建立了几何-因果基础。

title: MMR-Life: Piecing Together Real-life Scenes for Multimodal Multi-image Reasoning
summary: Recent progress in the reasoning capabilities of multimodal large language models (MLLMs) has empowered them to address more complex tasks such as scientific analysis and mathematical reasoning. Despite their promise, MLLMs' reasoning abilities across different scenarios in real life remain largely unexplored and lack standardized benchmarks for evaluation. To address this gap, we introduce MMR-Life, a comprehensive benchmark designed to evaluate the diverse multimodal multi-image reasoning capabilities of MLLMs across real-life scenarios. MMR-Life consists of 2,646 multiple-choice questions based on 19,108 images primarily sourced from real-world contexts, comprehensively covering seven reasoning types: abductive, analogical, causal, deductive, inductive, spatial, and temporal. Unlike existing reasoning benchmarks, MMR-Life does not rely on domain-specific expertise but instead requires models to integrate information across multiple images and apply diverse reasoning abilities. The evaluation of 37 advanced models highlights the substantial challenge posed by MMR-Life. Even top models like GPT-5 achieve only 58% accuracy and display considerable variance in performance across reasoning types. Moreover, we analyze the reasoning paradigms of existing MLLMs, exploring how factors such as thinking length, reasoning method, and reasoning type affect their performance. In summary, MMR-Life establishes a comprehensive foundation for evaluating, analyzing, and improving the next generation of multimodal reasoning systems.

AI.tldr: MMR-Life是一个评估多模态大语言模型在真实生活场景中多图像推理能力的综合基准，包含2,646个选择题和19,108张图像，覆盖7种推理类型。
AI.motivation: 尽管多模态大语言模型在科学分析和数学推理等复杂任务上取得进展，但其在真实生活场景中的推理能力尚未充分探索，缺乏标准化评估基准。
AI.method: 构建MMR-Life基准，包含2,646个基于19,108张真实世界图像的多选题，全面覆盖溯因、类比、因果、演绎、归纳、空间和时间等7种推理类型。
AI.result: 评估37个先进模型显示MMR-Life具有显著挑战性，即使顶级模型如GPT-5准确率仅58%，且在不同推理类型上表现差异很大。
AI.conclusion: MMR-Life为评估、分析和改进下一代多模态推理系统建立了全面基础，揭示了现有模型在真实生活多图像推理方面的局限性。

title: PARWiS: Winner determination under shoestring budgets using active pairwise comparisons
summary: Determining a winner among a set of items using active pairwise comparisons under a limited budget is a challenging problem in preference-based learning. The goal of this study is to implement and evaluate the PARWiS algorithm, which shows spectral ranking and disruptive pair selection to identify the best item under shoestring budgets. This work have extended the PARWiS with a contextual variant (Contextual PARWiS) and a reinforcement learning-based variant (RL PARWiS), comparing them against baselines, including Double Thompson Sampling and a random selection strategy. This evaluation spans synthetic and real-world datasets (Jester and MovieLens), using budgets of 40, 60, and 80 comparisons for 20 items. The performance is measured through recovery fraction, true rank of reported winner, reported rank of true winner, and cumulative regret, alongside the separation metric \(Δ_{1,2}\). Results show that PARWiS and RL PARWiS outperform baselines across all datasets, particularly in the Jester dataset with a higher \(Δ_{1,2}\), while performance gaps narrow in the more challenging MovieLens dataset with a smaller \(Δ_{1,2}\). Contextual PARWiS shows comparable performance to PARWiS, indicating that contextual features may require further tuning to provide significant benefits.

AI.tldr: PARWiS算法及其变体在有限预算下通过主动成对比较寻找最佳物品，在合成和真实数据集上优于基线方法。
AI.motivation: 在偏好学习中，如何在有限预算下通过主动成对比较确定最佳物品是一个具有挑战性的问题。需要开发有效的算法来在资源受限的情况下识别最优选项。
AI.method: 实现了PARWiS算法（结合谱排序和破坏性对选择），并扩展了上下文变体（Contextual PARWiS）和强化学习变体（RL PARWiS）。在20个物品上使用40、60、80次比较预算进行评估，对比基线包括Double Thompson Sampling和随机选择策略。
AI.result: PARWiS和RL PARWiS在所有数据集上均优于基线，特别是在Δ₁₂较高的Jester数据集上表现更佳。在Δ₁₂较小的MovieLens数据集上性能差距缩小。Contextual PARWiS与PARWiS性能相当，表明上下文特征可能需要进一步调优才能提供显著优势。
AI.conclusion: PARWiS算法及其强化学习变体在有限预算的主动成对比较任务中表现优异，特别是在区分度较大的数据集上。上下文特征的整合需要进一步优化才能发挥潜力。

title: EstLLM: Enhancing Estonian Capabilities in Multilingual LLMs via Continued Pretraining and Post-Training
summary: Large language models (LLMs) are predominantly trained on English-centric data, resulting in uneven performance for smaller languages. We study whether continued pretraining (CPT) can substantially improve Estonian capabilities in a pretrained multilingual LLM while preserving its English and general reasoning performance. Using Llama 3.1 8B as the main base model, we perform CPT on a mixture that increases Estonian exposure while approximating the original training distribution through English replay and the inclusion of code, mathematics, and instruction-like data. We subsequently apply supervised fine-tuning, preference optimization, and chat vector merging to introduce robust instruction-following behavior. Evaluation on a comprehensive suite of Estonian benchmarks shows consistent gains in linguistic competence, knowledge, reasoning, translation quality, and instruction-following compared to the original base model and its instruction-tuned variant, while maintaining competitive performance on English benchmarks. These findings indicate that CPT, with an appropriately balanced data mixture, together with post-training alignment, can substantially improve single-language capabilities in pretrained multilingual LLMs.

AI.tldr: 通过持续预训练和后续对齐技术，显著提升了多语言大语言模型在爱沙尼亚语上的能力，同时保持了英语性能。
AI.motivation: 当前大语言模型主要基于英语数据训练，导致对小语种（如爱沙尼亚语）的性能表现不均。研究旨在探索是否可以通过持续预训练显著提升预训练多语言模型在特定小语种上的能力，同时保持其英语和通用推理性能。
AI.method: 使用Llama 3.1 8B作为基础模型，进行持续预训练：采用混合数据策略增加爱沙尼亚语曝光度，同时通过英语回放、代码、数学和指令类数据近似原始训练分布。随后应用监督微调、偏好优化和聊天向量合并来引入稳健的指令跟随行为。
AI.result: 在全面的爱沙尼亚语基准测试中，相比原始基础模型及其指令调优变体，在语言能力、知识、推理、翻译质量和指令跟随方面均获得一致提升。同时在英语基准测试中保持竞争力。
AI.conclusion: 研究表明，通过适当平衡的数据混合和后续训练对齐，可以显著提升预训练多语言模型在单一语言上的能力，为解决小语种性能不均问题提供了有效方法。

title: Scaling of learning time for high dimensional inputs
summary: Representation learning from complex data typically involves models with a large number of parameters, which in turn require large amounts of data samples. In neural network models, model complexity grows with the number of inputs to each neuron, with a trade-off between model expressivity and learning time. A precise characterization of this trade-off would help explain the connectivity and learning times observed in artificial and biological networks. We present a theoretical analysis of how learning time depends on input dimensionality for a Hebbian learning model performing independent component analysis. Based on the geometry of high-dimensional spaces, we show that the learning dynamics reduce to a unidimensional problem, with learning times dependent only on initial conditions. For higher input dimensions, initial parameters have smaller learning gradients and larger learning times. We find that learning times have supralinear scaling, becoming quickly prohibitive for high input dimensions. These results reveal a fundamental limitation for learning in high dimensions and help elucidate how the optimal design of neural networks depends on data complexity. Our approach outlines a new framework for analyzing learning dynamics and model complexity in neural network models.

AI.tldr: 论文分析了高维输入下Hebbian学习模型的学习时间与输入维度之间的关系，发现学习时间随维度增加呈超线性增长，揭示了高维学习的基本限制。
AI.motivation: 复杂数据表示学习通常需要大量参数模型，而模型复杂度与每个神经元的输入数量相关，存在模型表达能力与学习时间之间的权衡。精确描述这种权衡有助于解释人工和生物神经网络中观察到的连接性和学习时间。
AI.method: 基于高维空间几何学，对执行独立成分分析的Hebbian学习模型进行理论分析，将学习动态简化为单维问题，研究学习时间如何依赖输入维度。
AI.result: 学习时间随输入维度增加呈超线性增长，高维输入时初始参数的学习梯度较小而学习时间较长，学习时间仅依赖于初始条件，高维学习存在基本限制。
AI.conclusion: 研究揭示了高维学习的基本限制，有助于阐明神经网络最优设计如何依赖于数据复杂度，并为分析神经网络模型的学习动态和模型复杂度提供了新框架。

title: What Exactly do Children Receive in Language Acquisition? A Case Study on CHILDES with Automated Detection of Filler-Gap Dependencies
summary: Children's acquisition of filler-gap dependencies has been argued by some to depend on innate grammatical knowledge, while others suggest that the distributional evidence available in child-directed speech suffices. Unfortunately, the relevant input is difficult to quantify at scale with fine granularity, making this question difficult to resolve. We present a system that identifies three core filler-gap constructions in spoken English corpora -- matrix wh-questions, embedded wh-questions, and relative clauses -- and further identifies the extraction site (i.e., subject vs. object vs. adjunct). Our approach combines constituency and dependency parsing, leveraging their complementary strengths for construction classification and extraction site identification. We validate the system on human-annotated data and find that it scores well across most categories. Applying the system to 57 English CHILDES corpora, we are able to characterize children's filler-gap input and their filler-gap production trajectories over the course of development, including construction-specific frequencies and extraction-site asymmetries. The resulting fine-grained labels enable future work in both acquisition and computational studies, which we demonstrate with a case study using filtered corpus training with language models.

AI.tldr: 开发了一个自动识别英语口语语料库中三种核心填充语-空位结构的系统，用于量化儿童语言输入中的分布证据，支持语言习得研究。
AI.motivation: 关于儿童习得填充语-空位依赖关系是依赖先天语法知识还是分布证据的争论，需要量化儿童导向语言中的相关输入，但现有方法难以大规模精细量化。
AI.method: 结合选区分析和依存分析的优势，开发系统识别英语口语中的三种核心填充语-空位结构（主句wh-问句、嵌入wh-问句、关系从句）及其提取位置（主语、宾语、附加语）。
AI.result: 系统在人工标注数据上表现良好，应用于57个英语CHILDES语料库，能够描述儿童填充语-空位输入及其产出轨迹，包括结构特定频率和提取位置不对称性。
AI.conclusion: 该系统为语言习得和计算研究提供了细粒度标注，通过语言模型的过滤语料训练案例展示了其应用价值，支持未来研究。

title: Operator Learning Using Weak Supervision from Walk-on-Spheres
summary: Training neural PDE solvers is often bottlenecked by expensive data generation or unstable physics-informed neural network (PINN) that involves challenging optimization landscapes due to higher-order derivatives. To tackle this issue, we propose an alternative approach using Monte Carlo approaches to estimate the solution to the PDE as a stochastic process for weak supervision during training. Leveraging the walk-on-spheres method, we introduce a learning scheme called \emph{Walk-on-Spheres Neural Operator (WoS-NO)} which uses weak supervision from WoS to train any given neural operator. We propose to amortize the cost of Monte Carlo walks across the distribution of PDE instances using stochastic representations from the WoS algorithm to generate cheap, noisy, estimates of the PDE solution during training. This is formulated into a data-free physics-informed objective where a neural operator is trained to regress against these weak supervisions, allowing the operator to learn a generalized solution map for an entire family of PDEs. This strategy results in a mesh-free framework that operates without expensive pre-computed datasets, avoids the need for computing higher-order derivatives for loss functions that are memory-intensive and unstable, and demonstrates zero-shot generalization to novel PDE parameters and domains. Experiments show that for the same number of training steps, our method exhibits up to 8.75$\times$ improvement in $L_2$-error compared to standard physics-informed training schemes, up to 6.31$\times$ improvement in training speed, and reductions of up to 2.97$\times$ in GPU memory consumption. We present the code at https://github.com/neuraloperator/WoS-NO

AI.tldr: 提出WoS-NO方法，使用蒙特卡洛游走球面法进行弱监督训练神经算子，无需昂贵数据集，避免高阶导数计算，实现零样本泛化
AI.motivation: 传统神经PDE求解器训练面临数据生成昂贵或PINN优化困难的问题，需要处理高阶导数带来的挑战性优化地形
AI.method: 使用蒙特卡洛方法估计PDE解作为随机过程进行弱监督训练，基于游走球面法提出WoS-NO学习方案，通过随机表示分摊蒙特卡洛游走成本
AI.result: 相比标准物理信息训练方案，L2误差提升8.75倍，训练速度提升6.31倍，GPU内存消耗减少2.97倍，实现零样本泛化
AI.conclusion: WoS-NO提供了一种无网格、无需预计算数据集、避免高阶导数计算的框架，显著提升训练效率和稳定性，实现泛化能力

title: Modeling Grammatical Hypothesis Testing in Young Learners: A Sequence-Based Learning Analytics Study of Morphosyntactic Reasoning in an Interactive Game
summary: This study investigates grammatical reasoning in primary school learners through a sequence-based learning analytics approach, leveraging fine-grained action sequences from an interactive game targeting morphosyntactic agreement in French. Unlike traditional assessments that rely on final answers, we treat each slider movement as a hypothesis-testing action, capturing real-time cognitive strategies during sentence construction. Analyzing 597 gameplay sessions (9,783 actions) from 100 students aged 8-11 in authentic classroom settings, we introduce Hamming distance to quantify proximity to valid grammatical solutions and examine convergence patterns across exercises with varying levels of difficulty. Results reveal that determiners and verbs are key sites of difficulty, with action sequences deviating from left-to-right usual treatment. This suggests learners often fix the verb first and adjust preceding elements. Exercises with fewer solutions exhibit slower and more erratic convergence, while changes in the closest valid solution indicate dynamic hypothesis revision. Our findings demonstrate how sequence-based analytics can uncover hidden dimensions of linguistic reasoning, offering a foundation for real-time scaffolding and teacher-facing tools in linguistically diverse classrooms.

AI.tldr: 研究通过基于序列的学习分析方法，利用法语形态句法一致性互动游戏中的细粒度动作序列，调查小学生的语法推理过程，发现学习者常先固定动词再调整前置成分，且解决方案较少的练习收敛更慢更不稳定。
AI.motivation: 传统语法评估主要依赖最终答案，无法捕捉学习者在句子构建过程中的实时认知策略。本研究旨在通过分析互动游戏中的动作序列，揭示语法推理的隐藏维度，为语言多样化课堂提供实时支架和教师工具。
AI.method: 采用基于序列的学习分析方法，分析100名8-11岁学生在真实课堂环境中597个游戏会话的9,783个动作。引入汉明距离量化与有效语法解决方案的接近程度，并检查不同难度练习的收敛模式。将每个滑块移动视为假设检验动作，捕捉句子构建过程中的实时认知策略。
AI.result: 结果显示：1) 限定词和动词是主要难点；2) 动作序列偏离从左到右的常规处理方式，表明学习者常先固定动词再调整前置元素；3) 解决方案较少的练习收敛更慢且更不稳定；4) 最近有效解决方案的变化表明动态假设修正过程。
AI.conclusion: 基于序列的分析方法能够揭示语言推理的隐藏维度，为语言多样化课堂中的实时支架和教师工具提供基础。该方法超越了传统评估的限制，能够捕捉语法学习过程中的动态认知策略。

title: Subliminal Signals in Preference Labels
summary: As AI systems approach superhuman capabilities, scalable oversight increasingly relies on LLM-as-a-judge frameworks where models evaluate and guide each other's training. A core assumption is that binary preference labels provide only semantic supervision about response quality. We challenge this assumption by demonstrating that preference labels can function as a covert communication channel. We show that even when a neutral student model generates semantically unbiased completions, a biased judge can transmit unintended behavioral traits through preference assignments, which even strengthen across iterative alignment rounds. Our findings suggest that robust oversight in superalignment settings requires mechanisms that can detect and mitigate subliminal preference transmission, particularly when judges may pursue unintended objectives.

AI.tldr: 研究发现偏好标签可作为隐蔽通信渠道，即使学生模型生成语义无偏的完成内容，有偏见的评判者仍能通过偏好分配传递意外行为特征，且这种传递在迭代对齐过程中会加强。
AI.motivation: 随着AI系统接近超人类能力，可扩展监督越来越依赖LLM作为评判者的框架。核心假设是二元偏好标签仅提供关于响应质量的语义监督。本文挑战这一假设，研究偏好标签是否可作为隐蔽通信渠道。
AI.method: 通过实验证明，即使中性学生模型生成语义无偏的完成内容，有偏见的评判者仍能通过偏好分配传递意外行为特征。研究这种传递在迭代对齐过程中的变化。
AI.result: 研究发现偏好标签确实可作为隐蔽通信渠道，有偏见的评判者能够通过偏好分配传递意外行为特征，且这种传递在迭代对齐过程中会加强。
AI.conclusion: 超级对齐设置中的鲁棒监督需要能够检测和减轻潜意识偏好传递的机制，特别是当评判者可能追求意外目标时。

title: ClinConsensus: A Consensus-Based Benchmark for Evaluating Chinese Medical LLMs across Difficulty Levels
summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly applied to health management, showing promise across disease prevention, clinical decision-making, and long-term care. However, existing medical benchmarks remain largely static and task-isolated, failing to capture the openness, longitudinal structure, and safety-critical complexity of real-world clinical workflows. We introduce ClinConsensus, a Chinese medical benchmark curated, validated, and quality-controlled by clinical experts. ClinConsensus comprises 2500 open-ended cases spanning the full continuum of care--from prevention and intervention to long-term follow-up--covering 36 medical specialties, 12 common clinical task types, and progressively increasing levels of complexity. To enable reliable evaluation of such complex scenarios, we adopt a rubric-based grading protocol and propose the Clinically Applicable Consistency Score (CACS@k). We further introduce a dual-judge evaluation framework, combining a high-capability LLM-as-judge with a distilled, locally deployable judge model trained via supervised fine-tuning, enabling scalable and reproducible evaluation aligned with physician judgment. Using ClinConsensus, we conduct a comprehensive assessment of several leading LLMs and reveal substantial heterogeneity across task themes, care stages, and medical specialties. While top-performing models achieve comparable overall scores, they differ markedly in reasoning, evidence use, and longitudinal follow-up capabilities, and clinically actionable treatment planning remains a key bottleneck. We release ClinConsensus as an extensible benchmark to support the development and evaluation of medical LLMs that are robust, clinically grounded, and ready for real-world deployment.

AI.tldr: ClinConsensus是中国首个由临床专家验证的医学基准，包含2500个开放式病例，覆盖36个专科和12种临床任务类型，采用双评委评估框架和CACS@k评分标准，用于评估医疗LLM在真实临床工作流中的表现。
AI.motivation: 现有医学基准大多是静态和任务孤立的，无法捕捉真实临床工作流的开放性、纵向结构和安全关键复杂性，需要开发更贴近实际临床场景的评估基准。
AI.method: 1) 构建ClinConsensus基准：包含2500个开放式病例，覆盖预防、干预到长期随访的全过程，涵盖36个专科和12种任务类型；2) 采用基于量表的评分协议和CACS@k评分标准；3) 引入双评委评估框架：结合高能力LLM评委和本地可部署的蒸馏评委模型。
AI.result: 对多个领先LLM的评估显示：1) 模型在不同任务主题、护理阶段和医学专科间表现存在显著异质性；2) 顶级模型总体得分相近，但在推理、证据使用和纵向随访能力方面差异明显；3) 临床可操作的治疗规划仍是关键瓶颈。
AI.conclusion: ClinConsensus作为可扩展的基准，支持开发评估稳健、临床基础扎实且适合真实世界部署的医疗LLM，揭示了当前模型在临床推理和纵向护理方面的局限性，为未来研究提供了方向。

title: Learn Hard Problems During RL with Reference Guided Fine-tuning
summary:
Reinforcement learning (RL) for mathematical reasoning can suffer from reward sparsity: for challenging problems, LLM fails to sample any correct trajectories, preventing RL from receiving meaningful positive feedback. At the same time, there often exist human-written reference solutions along with the problem (e.g., problems from AoPS), but directly fine-tuning on these solutions offers no benefit because models often cannot imitate human proofs that lie outside their own reasoning distribution.
  We introduce Reference-Guided Fine-Tuning (ReGFT), a simple and effective method that utilizes human-written reference solutions to synthesize positive trajectories on hard problems and train on them before RL. For each problem, we provide the model with a partial reference solution and let it generate its own reasoning trace, ensuring the resulting trajectories remain in the model's reasoning space while still benefiting from reference guidance.
  Fine-tuning on these reference-guided trajectories increases the number of solvable problems and produces a checkpoint that receives more positive rewards during RL. Across three benchmarks (AIME24, AIME25, BeyondAIME), ReGFT consistently improves supervised accuracy, accelerates DAPO training, and raises the final performance plateau of RL. Our results show that ReGFT effectively overcomes reward sparsity and unlocks stronger RL-based mathematical reasoning.

AI.tldr: ReGFT利用人类参考解答合成正样本轨迹，解决数学推理中强化学习的奖励稀疏问题，提升模型性能
AI.motivation: 数学推理中强化学习面临奖励稀疏问题：对于难题，LLM无法采样到任何正确轨迹，导致RL缺乏有意义的正反馈。虽然存在人类参考解答，但直接微调无效，因为模型无法模仿超出自身推理分布的人类证明。
AI.method: 提出Reference-Guided Fine-Tuning (ReGFT)：利用人类参考解答合成正样本轨迹。对每个问题，提供部分参考解答，让模型生成自己的推理轨迹，确保轨迹保持在模型的推理空间内，同时受益于参考指导。
AI.result: 在三个基准测试（AIME24、AIME25、BeyondAIME）上，ReGFT持续提升监督准确率，加速DAPO训练，提高RL最终性能平台。有效克服奖励稀疏问题，解锁更强的基于RL的数学推理能力。
AI.conclusion: ReGFT通过利用人类参考解答合成正样本轨迹，有效解决数学推理中强化学习的奖励稀疏问题，为RL训练提供更好的初始化，提升整体性能。

title: Recursive Think-Answer Process for LLMs and VLMs
summary: Think-Answer reasoners such as DeepSeek-R1 have made notable progress by leveraging interpretable internal reasoning. However, despite the frequent presence of self-reflective cues like "Oops!", they remain vulnerable to output errors during single-pass inference. To address this limitation, we propose an efficient Recursive Think-Answer Process (R-TAP) that enables models to engage in iterative reasoning cycles and generate more accurate answers, going beyond conventional single-pass approaches. Central to this approach is a confidence generator that evaluates the certainty of model responses and guides subsequent improvements. By incorporating two complementary rewards-Recursively Confidence Increase Reward and Final Answer Confidence Reward-we show that R-TAP-enhanced models consistently outperform conventional single-pass methods for both large language models (LLMs) and vision-language models (VLMs). Moreover, by analyzing the frequency of "Oops"-like expressions in model responses, we find that R-TAP-applied models exhibit significantly fewer self-reflective patterns, resulting in more stable and faster inference-time reasoning. We hope R-TAP pave the way evolving into efficient and elaborated methods to refine the reasoning processes of future AI.

AI.tldr: 提出R-TAP递归思维-回答过程，通过置信度评估和迭代推理循环改进单次推理的准确性，减少模型错误输出。
AI.motivation: 现有Think-Answer推理器（如DeepSeek-R1）虽然具有可解释的内部推理能力，但在单次推理过程中仍然容易产生输出错误，即使有"Oops!"等自我反思提示。需要一种超越传统单次推理的方法来提高准确性。
AI.method: 提出递归思维-回答过程（R-TAP），包含置信度生成器评估模型响应的确定性，并指导后续改进。通过两种互补奖励机制：递归置信度增加奖励和最终答案置信度奖励，实现迭代推理循环。
AI.result: R-TAP增强的模型在大型语言模型（LLMs）和视觉语言模型（VLMs）上都持续优于传统单次推理方法。应用R-TAP的模型表现出更少的自我反思模式（如"Oops"表达），推理更稳定快速。
AI.conclusion: R-TAP为未来AI推理过程的精炼提供了高效且精细化的方法路径，有望推动推理能力向更高效和准确的方向发展。

title: MOSAIC: A Unified Platform for Cross-Paradigm Comparison and Evaluation of Homogeneous and Heterogeneous Multi-Agent RL, LLM, VLM, and Human Decision-Makers
summary: Reinforcement learning (RL), large language models (LLMs), and vision-language models (VLMs) have been widely studied in isolation. However, existing infrastructure lacks the ability to deploy agents from different decision-making paradigms within the same environment, making it difficult to study them in hybrid multi-agent settings or to compare their behaviour fairly under identical conditions. We present MOSAIC, an open-source platform that bridges this gap by incorporating a diverse set of existing reinforcement learning environments and enabling heterogeneous agents (RL policies, LLMs, VLMs, and human players) to operate within them in ad-hoc team settings with reproducible results. MOSAIC introduces three contributions. (i) An IPC-based worker protocol that wraps both native and third-party frameworks as isolated subprocess workers, each executing its native training and inference logic unmodified, communicating through a versioned inter-process protocol. (ii) An operator abstraction that forms an agent-level interface by mapping workers to agents: each operator, regardless of whether it is backed by an RL policy, an LLM, or a human, conforms to a minimal unified interface. (iii) A deterministic cross-paradigm evaluation framework offering two complementary modes: a manual mode that advances up to N concurrent operators in lock-step under shared seeds for fine-grained visual inspection of behavioural differences, and a script mode that drives automated, long-running evaluation through declarative Python scripts, for reproducible experiments. We release MOSAIC as an open, visual-first platform to facilitate reproducible cross-paradigm research across the RL, LLM, and human-in-the-loop communities.

AI.tldr: MOSAIC是一个开源平台，支持在相同环境中部署不同决策范式的智能体（RL策略、LLM、VLM和人类玩家），实现异构智能体在混合多智能体设置中的可重复评估。
AI.motivation: 现有基础设施无法在同一环境中部署来自不同决策范式的智能体，难以在混合多智能体设置中研究它们，也无法在相同条件下公平比较它们的行为。
AI.method: 提出三个核心贡献：1) 基于IPC的工作者协议，将原生和第三方框架包装为隔离的子进程工作者；2) 操作者抽象，形成智能体级接口；3) 确定性跨范式评估框架，提供手动模式和脚本模式两种互补评估方式。
AI.result: 开发了MOSAIC平台，作为一个开放、视觉优先的平台，促进RL、LLM和人机交互社区之间的可重复跨范式研究。
AI.conclusion: MOSAIC填补了现有基础设施的空白，使异构智能体能够在相同环境中运行，支持可重复的跨范式研究，为RL、LLM和人类玩家之间的比较和协作提供了统一平台。

title: S2O: Enhancing Adversarial Training with Second-Order Statistics of Weights
summary: Adversarial training has emerged as a highly effective way to improve the robustness of deep neural networks (DNNs). It is typically conceptualized as a min-max optimization problem over model weights and adversarial perturbations, where the weights are optimized using gradient descent methods, such as SGD. In this paper, we propose a novel approach by treating model weights as random variables, which paves the way for enhancing adversarial training through \textbf{S}econd-Order \textbf{S}tatistics \textbf{O}ptimization (S$^2$O) over model weights. We challenge and relax a prevalent, yet often unrealistic, assumption in prior PAC-Bayesian frameworks: the statistical independence of weights. From this relaxation, we derive an improved PAC-Bayesian robust generalization bound. Our theoretical developments suggest that optimizing the second-order statistics of weights can substantially tighten this bound. We complement this theoretical insight by conducting an extensive set of experiments that demonstrate that S$^2$O not only enhances the robustness and generalization of neural networks when used in isolation, but also seamlessly augments other state-of-the-art adversarial training techniques. The code is available at https://github.com/Alexkael/S2O.

AI.tldr: 提出S²O方法，通过将模型权重视为随机变量并优化其二阶统计量来增强对抗训练，放松了权重独立假设，改进了PAC-Bayesian鲁棒泛化界。
AI.motivation: 对抗训练通常被概念化为模型权重和对抗扰动的最小-最大优化问题，但现有PAC-Bayesian框架中普遍假设权重统计独立，这一假设往往不现实。本文旨在放松这一假设，通过优化权重的二阶统计量来改进对抗训练。
AI.method: 提出S²O（二阶统计优化）方法：将模型权重视为随机变量，放松权重独立假设，推导改进的PAC-Bayesian鲁棒泛化界，并通过优化权重的二阶统计量来增强对抗训练。
AI.result: 理论分析表明优化权重二阶统计量可以显著收紧泛化界。实验证明S²O不仅能单独提升神经网络的鲁棒性和泛化能力，还能无缝增强其他最先进的对抗训练技术。
AI.conclusion: 通过将模型权重视为随机变量并优化其二阶统计量，S²O方法有效改进了对抗训练，放松了不现实的权重独立假设，在理论和实验上都取得了显著效果。

title: LongRLVR: Long-Context Reinforcement Learning Requires Verifiable Context Rewards
summary: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has significantly advanced the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) by optimizing them against factual outcomes. However, this paradigm falters in long-context scenarios, as its reliance on internal parametric knowledge is ill-suited for tasks requiring contextual grounding--the ability to find and reason over externally provided information. We identify a key reason for this failure: a reward based solely on the final answer is too sparse to effectively guide the model for identifying relevant evidence. We formally prove that the outcome-only reward leads to significant vanishing gradients for the context grounding process, rendering learning intractable. To overcome this bottleneck, we introduce LongRLVR to augment the sparse answer reward with a dense and verifiable context reward. This auxiliary signal directly incentivizes the model for selecting the correct grounding information, providing a robust learning gradient that solves the underlying optimization challenge. We validate our method on challenging long-context benchmarks using Qwen and LLaMA models. LongRLVR consistently and significantly outperforms the standard RLVR across all models and benchmarks, e.g., boosting a 14B model's scores on RULER-QA from 73.17 to 88.90 and on LongBench v2 from 39.8 to 46.5. Our work demonstrates that explicitly rewarding the grounding process is a critical and effective strategy for unlocking the full reasoning potential of LLMs in long-context applications. Our code is available at https://github.com/real-absolute-AI/LongRLVR.

AI.tldr: LongRLVR通过引入可验证的上下文奖励来解决长上下文场景中RLVR的稀疏奖励问题，显著提升大语言模型在长上下文任务中的表现。
AI.motivation: 传统的RLVR（基于可验证奖励的强化学习）在长上下文场景中表现不佳，因为它仅依赖最终答案的稀疏奖励，无法有效指导模型从外部上下文中找到相关证据进行推理。这导致在需要上下文基础的任务中学习困难。
AI.method: 提出LongRLVR方法，在稀疏的答案奖励基础上增加了密集且可验证的上下文奖励。这种辅助信号直接激励模型选择正确的上下文基础信息，为底层优化挑战提供了稳健的学习梯度。
AI.result: 在Qwen和LLaMA模型上的实验表明，LongRLVR在所有模型和基准测试中都显著优于标准RLVR。例如，14B模型在RULER-QA上的得分从73.17提升到88.90，在LongBench v2上从39.8提升到46.5。
AI.conclusion: 明确奖励上下文基础过程是解锁大语言模型在长上下文应用中完整推理潜力的关键且有效的策略。LongRLVR解决了标准RLVR在长上下文任务中的优化瓶颈。

title: GlassMol: Interpretable Molecular Property Prediction with Concept Bottleneck Models
summary: Machine learning accelerates molecular property prediction, yet state-of-the-art Large Language Models and Graph Neural Networks operate as black boxes. In drug discovery, where safety is critical, this opacity risks masking false correlations and excluding human expertise. Existing interpretability methods suffer from the effectiveness-trustworthiness trade-off: explanations may fail to reflect a model's true reasoning, degrade performance, or lack domain grounding. Concept Bottleneck Models (CBMs) offer a solution by projecting inputs to human-interpretable concepts before readout, ensuring that explanations are inherently faithful to the decision process. However, adapting CBMs to chemistry faces three challenges: the Relevance Gap (selecting task-relevant concepts from a large descriptor space), the Annotation Gap (obtaining concept supervision for molecular data), and the Capacity Gap (degrading performance due to bottleneck constraints). We introduce GlassMol, a model-agnostic CBM that addresses these gaps through automated concept curation and LLM-guided concept selection. Experiments across thirteen benchmarks demonstrate that \method generally matches or exceeds black-box baselines, suggesting that interpretability does not sacrifice performance and challenging the commonly assumed trade-off. Code is available at https://github.com/walleio/GlassMol.

AI.tldr: GlassMol是一个模型无关的概念瓶颈模型，通过自动概念筛选和LLM引导的概念选择解决化学分子预测中的可解释性挑战，在保持性能的同时提供忠实解释。
AI.motivation: 药物发现中安全至关重要，但当前机器学习模型（如大语言模型和图神经网络）作为黑箱运行，可能掩盖虚假相关性并排除人类专业知识。现有可解释性方法存在效果-可信度权衡问题。
AI.method: 提出GlassMol，一个模型无关的概念瓶颈模型，通过自动概念筛选和LLM引导的概念选择解决三个关键挑战：相关性差距（从大量描述符中选择任务相关概念）、标注差距（获取分子数据的概念监督）和容量差距（瓶颈约束导致的性能下降）。
AI.result: 在13个基准测试中，GlassMol通常匹配或超过黑箱基线模型，表明可解释性不一定牺牲性能，挑战了通常假设的权衡关系。
AI.conclusion: GlassMol通过解决概念瓶颈模型在化学领域的三个关键挑战，实现了在保持预测性能的同时提供忠实可解释性的目标，为药物发现中的安全关键应用提供了透明可靠的解决方案。

title: Zero- and Few-Shot Named-Entity Recognition: Case Study and Dataset in the Crime Domain (CrimeNER)
comment: Sent for review at the main conference of the International Conference of Document Analysis and Recognition (ICDAR) 2026
summary: The extraction of critical information from crime-related documents is a crucial task for law enforcement agencies. Named-Entity Recognition (NER) can perform this task in extracting information about the crime, the criminal, or law enforcement agencies involved. However, there is a considerable lack of adequately annotated data on general real-world crime scenarios. To address this issue, we present CrimeNER, a case-study of Crime-related zero- and Few-Shot NER, and a general Crime-related Named-Entity Recognition database (CrimeNERdb) consisting of more than 1.5k annotated documents for the NER task extracted from public reports on terrorist attacks and the U.S. Department of Justice's press notes. We define 5 types of coarse crime entity and a total of 22 types of fine-grained entity. We address the quality of the case-study and the annotated data with experiments on Zero and Few-Shot settings with State-of-the-Art NER models as well as generalist and commonly used Large Language Models.

AI.tldr: 提出CrimeNER：一个犯罪相关零样本和少样本NER案例研究，以及包含1500+标注文档的犯罪相关命名实体识别数据库(CrimeNERdb)，用于从犯罪文档中提取关键信息。
AI.motivation: 从犯罪相关文档中提取关键信息对执法机构至关重要，但缺乏针对现实世界犯罪场景的充分标注数据。
AI.method: 构建CrimeNERdb数据库，包含1500+个从恐怖袭击公开报告和美国司法部新闻稿中提取的标注文档；定义5种粗粒度犯罪实体和22种细粒度实体类型；在零样本和少样本设置下使用最先进的NER模型以及通用大型语言模型进行实验。
AI.result: 创建了一个专门针对犯罪领域的NER数据库，并通过实验验证了在零样本和少样本设置下使用现有模型进行犯罪实体识别的可行性。
AI.conclusion: CrimeNER和CrimeNERdb填补了犯罪领域标注数据的空白，为执法机构的信息提取任务提供了有价值的资源和基准。

title: The Impact of Battery Cell Configuration on Electric Vehicle Performance: An XGBoost-Based Classification with SHAP Interpretability
summary: As the electric vehicle (EV) market continues to prioritize dynamic performance and rapid charging, battery configuration has rapidly evolved. Despite this, current literature has often overlooked the complex, non-linear relationship between battery configuration and electric vehicle performance. To address this gap, this study proposes a machine learning framework which categorizes the EV acceleration performance into High (<= 4.0 seconds), Mid (4.0 - 7.0 seconds), and Low (> 7.0 seconds). Utilizing a preprocessed dataset consisting of 276 EV samples, an Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) classifier was utilized, achieving 87.5% predictive accuracy, a 0.968 ROC-AUC, and a 0.812 MCC. In order to ensure engineering transparency SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) were employed. Results of analysis shows that an increase in battery cell count initially boosts power delivery, but its mass and complexity diminished performance gains eventually. As such, these findings indicate that battery configuration in EVs must balance system complexity and architectural configuration in order to receive and retain optimal vehicle performance.

AI.tldr: 本研究提出机器学习框架，使用XGBoost分类器预测电动车加速性能（高/中/低三档），准确率达87.5%，并通过SHAP分析揭示电池配置与性能的非线性关系。
AI.motivation: 当前电动车市场重视动态性能和快速充电，电池配置快速发展，但现有文献常忽视电池配置与电动车性能之间复杂的非线性关系，需要填补这一研究空白。
AI.method: 将EV加速性能分为高（≤4.0秒）、中（4.0-7.0秒）、低（>7.0秒）三档；使用包含276个EV样本的预处理数据集；采用极端梯度提升（XGBoost）分类器；使用SHAP方法确保工程透明度。
AI.result: XGBoost分类器达到87.5%预测准确率，ROC-AUC为0.968，MCC为0.812；SHAP分析显示电池单元数量增加初期提升功率输出，但质量和复杂性最终会削弱性能增益。
AI.conclusion: 电动车电池配置需要在系统复杂性和架构配置之间取得平衡，以实现并保持最佳车辆性能；电池单元数量的增加存在收益递减点。

title: Organizing, Orchestrating, and Benchmarking Agent Skills at Ecosystem Scale
summary: The rapid proliferation of Claude agent skills has raised the central question of how to effectively leverage, manage, and scale the agent skill ecosystem. In this paper, we propose AgentSkillOS, the first principled framework for skill selection, orchestration, and ecosystem-level management. AgentSkillOS comprises two stages: (i) Manage Skills, which organizes skills into a capability tree via node-level recursive categorization for efficient discovery; and (ii) Solve Tasks, which retrieves, orchestrates, and executes multiple skills through DAG-based pipelines. To evaluate the agent's ability to invoke skills, we construct a benchmark of 30 artifact-rich tasks across five categories: data computation, document creation, motion video, visual design, and web interaction. We assess the quality of task outputs using LLM-based pairwise evaluation, and the results are aggregated via a Bradley-Terry model to produce unified quality scores. Experiments across three skill ecosystem scales (200 to 200K skills) show that tree-based retrieval effectively approximates oracle skill selection, and that DAG-based orchestration substantially outperforms native flat invocation even when given the identical skill set. Our findings confirm that structured composition is the key to unlocking skill potential. Our GitHub repository is available at:https://github.com/ynulihao/AgentSkillOS.

AI.tldr: AgentSkillOS是一个用于Claude智能体技能管理的框架，通过能力树组织和DAG管道执行来有效管理大规模技能生态系统。
AI.motivation: 随着Claude智能体技能的快速增加，如何有效利用、管理和扩展技能生态系统成为一个核心问题。需要系统化的方法来处理技能的选择、编排和生态系统级管理。
AI.method: 提出AgentSkillOS框架，包含两个阶段：1) 管理技能：通过节点级递归分类将技能组织成能力树，便于高效发现；2) 解决任务：通过DAG管道检索、编排和执行多个技能。使用基于LLM的成对评估和Bradley-Terry模型进行质量评估。
AI.result: 实验在三个技能生态系统规模（200到20万技能）上进行，结果显示：基于树的检索能有效近似oracle技能选择，基于DAG的编排即使使用相同技能集也显著优于原生扁平调用。结构化组合是释放技能潜力的关键。
AI.conclusion: AgentSkillOS为智能体技能生态系统提供了首个原则性框架，通过结构化组织和管理方法，能够有效处理大规模技能的选择和编排问题，显著提升任务执行质量。

title: Attention Smoothing Is All You Need For Unlearning
summary: Large Language Models are prone to memorizing sensitive, copyrighted, or hazardous content, posing significant privacy and legal concerns. Retraining from scratch is computationally infeasible, whereas current unlearning methods exhibit unstable trade-offs between forgetting and utility, frequently producing incoherent outputs on forget prompts and failing to generalize due to the persistence of lexical-level and semantic-level associations in attention. We propose Attention Smoothing Unlearning (ASU), a principled framework that casts unlearning as self-distillation from a forget-teacher derived from the model's own attention. By increasing the softmax temperature, ASU flattens attention distributions and directly suppresses the lexical-level and semantic-level associations responsible for reconstructing memorized knowledge. This results in a bounded optimization objective that erases factual information yet maintains coherence in responses to forget prompts. Empirical evaluation on TOFU, MUSE, and WMDP, along with real-world and continual unlearning scenarios across question answering and text completion, demonstrates that ASU outperforms the baselines for most unlearning scenarios, delivering robust unlearning with minimal loss of model utility.

AI.tldr: ASU通过注意力平滑蒸馏框架，直接抑制词汇和语义关联来消除记忆内容，在保持模型效用的同时实现稳健的遗忘
AI.motivation: 大语言模型容易记忆敏感、受版权保护或有危害的内容，带来隐私和法律问题。重新训练计算成本过高，现有遗忘方法在遗忘和效用之间存在不稳定权衡，经常产生不连贯输出，且由于注意力中词汇和语义关联的持续存在而无法泛化。
AI.method: 提出注意力平滑遗忘(ASU)框架，将遗忘视为从模型自身注意力派生的遗忘教师进行自蒸馏。通过增加softmax温度来平滑注意力分布，直接抑制重建记忆知识所需的词汇和语义关联，形成有界优化目标。
AI.result: 在TOFU、MUSE、WMDP数据集以及现实世界和持续遗忘场景的实证评估表明，ASU在大多数遗忘场景中优于基线方法，实现了稳健的遗忘且模型效用损失最小。
AI.conclusion: ASU通过注意力平滑蒸馏有效解决了大语言模型记忆敏感内容的问题，在保持模型连贯性和效用的同时实现了可靠的遗忘，为模型隐私和安全提供了实用解决方案。

title: Reasoning Core: A Scalable Procedural Data Generation Suite for Symbolic Pre-training and Post-Training
comment: Keywords: LLMs, NLP, Dataset, Corpus, Procedural Pre-training, Reasoning, Logic, Formal Semantics https://github.com/sileod/reasoning_core
summary: Training on verifiable symbolic data is a promising way to expand the reasoning frontier of language models beyond what standard pre-training corpora provide. Yet existing procedural generators often rely on fixed puzzles or templates and do not deliver the distributional breadth needed at scale. We introduce Reasoning Core, a scalable suite that procedurally generates verifiable symbolic reasoning data across core formal domains: PDDL planning over randomized domains, first-order logic with equality, context-free grammar parsing and generation, causal reasoning over random Bayesian networks, and systems of equations. Each task is paired with an external solver for rigorous verification and admits continuous difficulty control for curriculum design. Examples can optionally include solver-derived reasoning traces, enabling supervised training from the earliest pre-training stages, and the same interface provides verifiable reward functions for reinforcement learning. Our experiments show that mixing Reasoning Core data into pre-training improves downstream reasoning while preserving, or slightly improving, language modeling quality. Zero-shot evaluations confirm these tasks challenge frontier models such as GPT-5. The code and data are publicly available under the MIT license.

AI.tldr: Reasoning Core是一个可扩展的符号推理数据生成套件，覆盖多个形式化领域，通过外部求解器验证，支持课程设计和强化学习，提升语言模型的推理能力。
AI.motivation: 现有过程生成器通常依赖固定谜题或模板，无法提供大规模所需的分布广度，需要一种可扩展的方法来生成可验证的符号推理数据。
AI.method: 引入Reasoning Core套件，过程化生成覆盖PDDL规划、一阶逻辑、上下文无关文法、贝叶斯网络因果推理和方程组等多个核心形式化领域的可验证符号推理数据，每个任务都配有外部求解器进行严格验证，支持难度连续控制。
AI.result: 将Reasoning Core数据混合到预训练中可以提高下游推理能力，同时保持或略微改善语言建模质量。零样本评估表明这些任务对GPT-5等前沿模型具有挑战性。
AI.conclusion: Reasoning Core提供了一个可扩展的框架，用于生成可验证的符号推理数据，支持从早期预训练阶段开始的监督学习和强化学习，是扩展语言模型推理前沿的有效方法。

title: JailNewsBench: Multi-Lingual and Regional Benchmark for Fake News Generation under Jailbreak Attacks
summary: Fake news undermines societal trust and decision-making across politics, economics, health, and international relations, and in extreme cases threatens human lives and societal safety. Because fake news reflects region-specific political, social, and cultural contexts and is expressed in language, evaluating the risks of large language models (LLMs) requires a multi-lingual and regional perspective. Malicious users can bypass safeguards through jailbreak attacks, inducing LLMs to generate fake news. However, no benchmark currently exists to systematically assess attack resilience across languages and regions. Here, we propose JailNewsBench, the first benchmark for evaluating LLM robustness against jailbreak-induced fake news generation. JailNewsBench spans 34 regions and 22 languages, covering 8 evaluation sub-metrics through LLM-as-a-Judge and 5 jailbreak attacks, with approximately 300k instances. Our evaluation of 9 LLMs reveals that the maximum attack success rate (ASR) reached 86.3% and the maximum harmfulness score was 3.5 out of 5. Notably, for English and U.S.-related topics, the defensive performance of typical multi-lingual LLMs was significantly lower than for other regions, highlighting substantial imbalances in safety across languages and regions. In addition, our analysis shows that coverage of fake news in existing safety datasets is limited and less well defended than major categories such as toxicity and social bias. Our dataset and code are available at https://github.com/kanekomasahiro/jail_news_bench.

AI.tldr: JailNewsBench是首个评估大语言模型对抗越狱攻击生成假新闻的鲁棒性基准，涵盖34个地区、22种语言，包含约30万实例，发现最大攻击成功率可达86.3%，且多语言模型在英语和美国相关话题上的防御性能显著低于其他地区。
AI.motivation: 假新闻威胁社会信任和决策安全，而现有基准缺乏从多语言和地区角度系统评估大语言模型对抗越狱攻击生成假新闻的鲁棒性。
AI.method: 提出JailNewsBench基准，涵盖34个地区、22种语言，通过LLM-as-a-Judge评估8个子指标，使用5种越狱攻击方法，构建约30万实例的数据集。
AI.result: 评估9个大语言模型显示：最大攻击成功率达86.3%，最大危害评分3.5/5；多语言模型在英语和美国相关话题上的防御性能显著低于其他地区；现有安全数据集对假新闻的覆盖有限且防御较弱。
AI.conclusion: 需要开发更平衡的多语言安全防御机制，现有大语言模型在假新闻生成方面存在显著安全漏洞，特别是在英语和美国相关话题上。

title: Integrating LTL Constraints into PPO for Safe Reinforcement Learning
summary: This paper proposes Proximal Policy Optimization with Linear Temporal Logic Constraints (PPO-LTL), a framework that integrates safety constraints written in LTL into PPO for safe reinforcement learning. LTL constraints offer rigorous representations of complex safety requirements, such as regulations that broadly exist in robotics, enabling systematic monitoring of safety requirements. Violations against LTL constraints are monitored by limit-deterministic Büchi automata, and then translated by a logic-to-cost mechanism into penalty signals. The signals are further employed for guiding the policy optimization via the Lagrangian scheme. Extensive experiments on the Zones and CARLA environments show that our PPO-LTL can consistently reduce safety violations, while maintaining competitive performance, against the state-of-the-art methods. The code is at https://github.com/EVIEHub/PPO-LTL.

AI.tldr: PPO-LTL框架将线性时序逻辑约束集成到PPO中，用于安全强化学习，通过自动机监控约束违反并转化为惩罚信号，在保持性能的同时显著减少安全违规。
AI.motivation: 机器人等领域存在复杂的安全约束需求，需要将形式化的安全规范集成到强化学习中，确保智能体在满足复杂安全要求的同时学习有效策略。
AI.method: 使用线性时序逻辑表达安全约束，通过极限确定性Büchi自动机监控约束违反，采用逻辑到成本的转换机制将违反转化为惩罚信号，结合拉格朗日方案指导策略优化。
AI.result: 在Zones和CARLA环境中的实验表明，PPO-LTL相比最先进方法能持续减少安全违规，同时保持竞争性的性能表现。
AI.conclusion: PPO-LTL成功将形式化安全约束集成到强化学习框架中，为复杂安全要求的机器人应用提供了有效的解决方案，代码已开源。

title: I Can't Believe It's Not Robust: Catastrophic Collapse of Safety Classifiers under Embedding Drift
comment: Accepted at the ICBINB: Where LLMs Need to Improve workshop at ICLR 2026. 12 pages and 3 Figures
summary: Instruction tuned reasoning models are increasingly deployed with safety classifiers trained on frozen embeddings, assuming representation stability across model updates. We systematically investigate this assumption and find it fails: normalized perturbations of magnitude $σ=0.02$ (corresponding to $\approx 1^\circ$ angular drift on the embedding sphere) reduce classifier performance from $85\%$ to $50\%$ ROC-AUC. Critically, mean confidence only drops $14\%$, producing dangerous silent failures where $72\%$ of misclassifications occur with high confidence, defeating standard monitoring. We further show that instruction-tuned models exhibit 20$\%$ worse class separability than base models, making aligned systems paradoxically harder to safeguard. Our findings expose a fundamental fragility in production AI safety architectures and challenge the assumption that safety mechanisms transfer across model versions.

AI.tldr: 研究发现，基于冻结嵌入的安全分类器在模型更新时存在表示不稳定性问题，即使微小的嵌入扰动也会导致分类器性能大幅下降，但置信度下降不明显，造成危险的静默失败。
AI.motivation: 当前AI安全部署中普遍假设指令调优推理模型的嵌入表示在不同版本间保持稳定，但这一假设未经系统验证。研究者希望检验这一关键假设，以评估现有安全架构的鲁棒性。
AI.method: 系统研究嵌入表示稳定性假设，通过引入归一化扰动（σ=0.02，约1度角漂移）测试分类器性能变化，分析置信度变化模式，并比较指令调优模型与基础模型的类别可分性差异。
AI.result: 微小扰动使分类器ROC-AUC从85%降至50%，但平均置信度仅下降14%，导致72%的错误分类发生在高置信度下，形成静默失败。指令调优模型的类别可分性比基础模型差20%。
AI.conclusion: AI安全架构存在根本性脆弱性，安全机制不能简单跨模型版本迁移，指令调优模型虽然对齐但更难保障安全，需要重新思考生产环境中的安全监控策略。

title: Provable and Practical In-Context Policy Optimization for Self-Improvement
comment: 34 pages, 8 tables, 4 figures, Accepted by ICLR 2026
summary: We study test-time scaling, where a model improves its answer through multi-round self-reflection at inference. We introduce In-Context Policy Optimization (ICPO), in which an agent optimizes its response in context using self-assessed or externally observed rewards without modifying its parameters. To explain this ICPO process, we theoretically show that with sufficient pretraining under a novel Fisher-weighted logit-matching objective, a single-layer linear self-attention model can provably imitate policy-optimization algorithm for linear bandits. Building on this theory, we propose Minimum-Entropy ICPO (ME-ICPO), a practical algorithm that iteratively uses its response and self-assessed reward to refine its response in-context at inference time. By selecting the responses and their rewards with minimum entropy, ME-ICPO ensures the robustness of the self-assessed rewards via majority voting. Across standard mathematical reasoning tasks, ME-ICPO attains competitive, top-tier performance while keeping inference costs affordable compared with other inference-time algorithms. Overall, ICPO provides a principled understanding of self-reflection in LLMs and yields practical benefits for test-time scaling for mathematical reasoning.

AI.tldr: 论文提出ICPO（上下文策略优化），一种无需修改模型参数、在推理时通过多轮自我反思优化响应的测试时缩放方法，并基于理论分析开发了实用的ME-ICPO算法，在数学推理任务上取得优异性能。
AI.motivation: 当前大语言模型在推理时通过自我反思改进答案的能力缺乏理论解释，且现有方法效率不高。本文旨在为LLMs的自我反思提供理论依据，并开发高效的测试时优化方法。
AI.method: 提出ICPO框架，通过理论证明单层线性自注意力模型在Fisher加权logit匹配目标下可模拟线性bandit的策略优化算法。基于此提出ME-ICPO算法，通过最小熵选择和多数投票机制迭代优化响应。
AI.result: 在标准数学推理任务上，ME-ICPO取得了具有竞争力的顶级性能，同时保持了可负担的推理成本，优于其他推理时算法。
AI.conclusion: ICPO为LLMs的自我反思提供了理论解释，ME-ICPO作为其实用实现，在数学推理任务上展示了测试时缩放的实际价值，平衡了性能与效率。

title: UTICA: Multi-Objective Self-Distllation Foundation Model Pretraining for Time Series Classification
summary: Self-supervised foundation models have achieved remarkable success across domains, including time series. However, the potential of non-contrastive methods, a paradigm that has driven significant advances in computer vision, remains underexplored for time series. In this work, we adapt DINOv2-style self-distillation to pretrain a time series foundation model, building on the Mantis tokenizer and transformer encoder architecture as our backbone. Through a student-teacher framework, our method Utica learns representations that capture both temporal invariance via augmented crops and fine-grained local structure via patch masking. Our approach achieves state-of-the-art classification performance on both UCR and UEA benchmarks. These results suggest that non-contrastive methods are a promising and complementary pretraining strategy for time series foundation models.

AI.tldr: 将DINOv2风格的自蒸馏方法应用于时间序列预训练，通过学生-教师框架学习时间不变性和局部结构，在UCR和UEA基准上达到SOTA分类性能
AI.motivation: 非对比学习方法在计算机视觉领域取得了显著成功，但在时间序列领域尚未充分探索。本文旨在将DINOv2风格的自蒸馏方法应用于时间序列预训练，探索非对比方法作为时间序列基础模型的补充预训练策略的潜力。
AI.method: 基于Mantis分词器和Transformer编码器架构，采用DINOv2风格的自蒸馏方法。通过学生-教师框架，利用增强裁剪学习时间不变性，通过补丁掩码学习细粒度局部结构。
AI.result: 在UCR和UEA基准测试中实现了最先进的分类性能，表明非对比方法是时间序列基础模型的有前景的补充预训练策略。
AI.conclusion: 非对比学习方法（特别是自蒸馏方法）是时间序列基础模型的有前景且互补的预训练策略，能够有效学习时间序列的表示。

title: Constructing Synthetic Instruction Datasets for Improving Reasoning in Domain-Specific LLMs: A Case Study in the Japanese Financial Domain
comment: 8 pages, 2 figures. Japanese version published in NLP2026
summary: In adapting LLMs to specific domains, achieving both domain expertise and reasoning ability remains an urgent challenge. This study proposes a general method for constructing high-quality synthetic instruction data for any domain, starting from domain-specific vocabulary. As a demonstration, we applied this method to the financial domain and constructed a large-scale instruction dataset totaling approximately 9.5 billion tokens with Chain-of-Thought reasoning traces. Evaluation results confirmed performance improvements over baseline models on financial benchmarks, demonstrating the effectiveness of our approach. We also report findings on the impact of reasoning trace length on performance and its limitations. Lastly, we open-source our models and datasets on https://huggingface.co/nri-ai .

AI.tldr: 提出一种基于领域特定词汇构建高质量合成指令数据的通用方法，应用于金融领域创建了包含约95亿token的大规模指令数据集，并开源了模型和数据集。
AI.motivation: 在将大语言模型适配到特定领域时，如何同时保持领域专业知识和推理能力是一个紧迫的挑战。现有方法难以平衡这两方面的需求。
AI.method: 提出一种通用方法：从领域特定词汇出发，构建高质量的合成指令数据。以金融领域为例，创建了包含Chain-of-Thought推理轨迹的大规模指令数据集（约95亿token）。
AI.result: 在金融基准测试中，使用该方法训练的模型性能优于基线模型。同时研究了推理轨迹长度对性能的影响及其局限性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法能有效提升LLM在特定领域的专业知识和推理能力，为领域适配提供了可行的解决方案。已开源模型和数据集。

title: Fed-GAME: Personalized Federated Learning with Graph Attention Mixture-of-Experts For Time-Series Forecasting
summary: Federated learning (FL) on graphs shows promise for distributed time-series forecasting. Yet, existing methods rely on static topologies and struggle with client heterogeneity. We propose Fed-GAME, a framework that models personalized aggregation as message passing over a learnable dynamic implicit graph. The core is a decoupled parameter difference-based update protocol, where clients transmit parameter differences between their fine-tuned private model and a shared global model. On the server, these differences are decomposed into two streams: (1) averaged difference used to updating the global model for consensus (2) the selective difference fed into a novel Graph Attention Mixture-of-Experts (GAME) aggregator for fine-grained personalization. In this aggregator, shared experts provide scoring signals while personalized gates adaptively weight selective updates to support personalized aggregation. Experiments on two real-world electric vehicle charging datasets demonstrate that Fed-GAME outperforms state-of-the-art personalized FL baselines.

AI.tldr: Fed-GAME：一种用于图联邦学习的动态隐式图框架，通过解耦参数差异更新协议和图注意力专家混合聚合器，实现个性化聚合，提升时间序列预测性能。
AI.motivation: 现有的图联邦学习方法依赖静态拓扑结构，难以处理客户端异构性问题，限制了分布式时间序列预测的性能。
AI.method: 提出Fed-GAME框架，将个性化聚合建模为可学习动态隐式图上的消息传递。核心是解耦参数差异更新协议：客户端传输其微调私有模型与共享全局模型之间的参数差异；服务器端将差异分解为两个流：(1)平均差异用于更新全局模型达成共识，(2)选择性差异输入新颖的图注意力专家混合(GAME)聚合器进行细粒度个性化。该聚合器中，共享专家提供评分信号，个性化门自适应加权选择性更新以支持个性化聚合。
AI.result: 在两个真实世界电动汽车充电数据集上的实验表明，Fed-GAME优于最先进的个性化联邦学习基线方法。
AI.conclusion: Fed-GAME通过动态隐式图建模和细粒度个性化聚合机制，有效解决了图联邦学习中的客户端异构性问题，提升了分布式时间序列预测性能。

title: DUEL: Exact Likelihood for Masked Diffusion via Deterministic Unmasking
summary: Masked diffusion models (MDMs) generate text by iteratively selecting positions to unmask and then predicting tokens at those positions. Yet MDMs lack proper perplexity evaluation: the ELBO is a loose bound on likelihood under the training distribution, not the test-time distribution, while generative perplexity requires a biased external model and ignores diversity. To address this, we introduce the \textsc{DUEL} framework, which formalizes \emph{deterministic} position selection, unifying leading MDM sampling strategies. We prove \textbf{\textsc{DUEL} admits \emph{exact} likelihood computation} via a simple algorithm, evaluated under the same position selection used at test time. This \textbf{gives MDMs proper perplexity for the first time} -- the natural analogue of autoregressive perplexity. With proper perplexity in hand, we revisit key questions about MDMs. \textbf{MDMs are substantially better than previously thought}: the MDM-autoregressive perplexity gap shrinks by up to 32\% on in-domain data and 82\% on zero-shot benchmarks. \textsc{DUEL} enables the first principled comparison of fast, parallel samplers across compute budgets -- an analysis impossible with the ELBO and unreliable with generative perplexity -- identifying probability margin \citep{kim2025train} as a strong default. Finally, oracle search over position orderings reveals MDMs can far surpass autoregressive models -- achieving 36.47 vs.\ 52.11 perplexity on AG News -- demonstrating the ceiling of MDM performance has not yet been reached.

AI.tldr: DUEL框架首次为掩码扩散模型提供了准确的可能性计算，显著缩小了MDM与自回归模型的困惑度差距，并揭示了MDM性能上限尚未达到。
AI.motivation: 掩码扩散模型缺乏适当的困惑度评估方法：ELBO是训练分布下的宽松边界，而生成困惑度需要有偏的外部模型且忽略多样性。
AI.method: 提出DUEL框架，形式化确定性位置选择，统一主流MDM采样策略，并证明DUEL允许通过简单算法进行准确可能性计算。
AI.result: MDM性能远超预期：MDM与自回归模型的困惑度差距在领域内数据上缩小32%，在零样本基准上缩小82%；概率边缘采样表现优异；MDM性能上限可达36.47困惑度（AG News）。
AI.conclusion: DUEL首次为MDM提供了适当的困惑度评估，揭示了MDM性能被低估，且其性能上限尚未达到，为MDM的进一步发展提供了新方向。

title: Causal Neural Probabilistic Circuits
summary: Concept Bottleneck Models (CBMs) enhance the interpretability of end-to-end neural networks by introducing a layer of concepts and predicting the class label from the concept predictions. A key property of CBMs is that they support interventions, i.e., domain experts can correct mispredicted concept values at test time to improve the final accuracy. However, typical CBMs apply interventions by overwriting only the corrected concept while leaving other concept predictions unchanged, which ignores causal dependencies among concepts. To address this, we propose the Causal Neural Probabilistic Circuit (CNPC), which combines a neural attribute predictor with a causal probabilistic circuit compiled from a causal graph. This circuit supports exact, tractable causal inference that inherently respects causal dependencies. Under interventions, CNPC models the class distribution based on a Product of Experts (PoE) that fuses the attribute predictor's predictive distribution with the interventional marginals computed by the circuit. We theoretically characterize the compositional interventional error of CNPC w.r.t. its modules and identify conditions under which CNPC closely matches the ground-truth interventional class distribution. Experiments on five benchmark datasets in both in-distribution and out-of-distribution settings show that, compared with five baseline models, CNPC achieves higher task accuracy across different numbers of intervened attributes.

AI.tldr: CNPC结合神经属性预测器和因果概率电路，通过乘积专家方法融合预测分布与因果干预边际分布，在概念干预时考虑概念间的因果依赖关系，提升任务准确率。
AI.motivation: 传统概念瓶颈模型在干预时只覆盖修正的概念，忽略概念间的因果依赖关系，这限制了干预效果。需要一种能考虑因果依赖的干预方法。
AI.method: 提出因果神经概率电路（CNPC），结合神经属性预测器和从因果图编译的因果概率电路。使用乘积专家方法融合属性预测器的预测分布与电路计算的干预边际分布。
AI.result: 在五个基准数据集上的实验表明，与五个基线模型相比，CNPC在不同干预属性数量下都取得了更高的任务准确率，包括分布内和分布外设置。
AI.conclusion: CNPC通过考虑概念间的因果依赖关系，在概念干预时能更好地匹配真实干预类别分布，显著提升了概念瓶颈模型的干预效果和任务准确率。

title: One Operator to Rule Them All? On Boundary-Indexed Operator Families in Neural PDE Solvers
comment: Accepted to the ICLR 2026 Workshop on AI & PDEs. 10 pages, 5 figures
summary: Neural PDE solvers are often described as learning solution operators that map problem data to PDE solutions. In this work, we argue that this interpretation is generally incorrect when boundary conditions vary. We show that standard neural operator training implicitly learns a boundary-indexed family of operators, rather than a single boundary-agnostic operator, with the learned mapping fundamentally conditioned on the boundary-condition distribution seen during training. We formalize this perspective by framing operator learning as conditional risk minimization over boundary conditions, which leads to a non-identifiability result outside the support of the training boundary distribution. As a consequence, generalization in forcing terms or resolution does not imply generalization across boundary conditions. We support our theoretical analysis with controlled experiments on the Poisson equation, demonstrating sharp degradation under boundary-condition shifts, cross-distribution failures between distinct boundary ensembles, and convergence to conditional expectations when boundary information is removed. Our results clarify a core limitation of current neural PDE solvers and highlight the need for explicit boundary-aware modeling in the pursuit of foundation models for PDEs.

AI.tldr: 神经PDE求解器通常声称学习从问题数据到PDE解的操作符，但本文指出当边界条件变化时这种解释是错误的。标准神经算子训练实际上学习的是边界条件索引的操作符族，而非单一边界无关的操作符，导致在训练边界分布之外无法泛化。
AI.motivation: 当前神经PDE求解器通常被描述为学习从问题数据到PDE解的操作符，但作者认为当边界条件变化时这种解释是不正确的。需要澄清神经算子训练的实际机制，揭示其在边界条件变化时的局限性，为构建真正的PDE基础模型提供理论指导。
AI.method: 将算子学习框架化为边界条件上的条件风险最小化问题，推导出训练边界分布之外的非可识别性结果。在泊松方程上进行控制实验，测试边界条件偏移下的性能退化、不同边界集合间的跨分布失败，以及移除边界信息时向条件期望的收敛。
AI.result: 实验表明神经PDE求解器在边界条件偏移下性能急剧下降，在不同边界集合间出现跨分布失败，移除边界信息时收敛到条件期望。这证实了标准神经算子训练学习的是边界条件索引的操作符族，而非单一边界无关操作符。
AI.conclusion: 当前神经PDE求解器存在核心局限性：它们学习的是边界条件分布上的条件映射，而非真正的边界无关操作符。在训练边界分布之外无法泛化，强制项或分辨率的泛化不意味着边界条件的泛化。需要明确的边界感知建模来构建真正的PDE基础模型。

title: Tackling multiphysics problems via finite element-guided physics-informed operator learning
summary: This work presents a finite element-guided physics-informed operator learning framework for multiphysics problems with coupled partial differential equations (PDEs) on arbitrary domains. Implemented with Folax, a JAX-based operator-learning platform, the proposed framework learns a mapping from the input parameter space to the solution space with a weighted residual formulation based on the finite element method, enabling discretization-independent prediction beyond the training resolution without relying on labaled simulation data. The present framework for multiphysics problems is verified on nonlinear thermo-mechanical problems. Two- and three-dimensional representative volume elements with varying heterogeneous microstructures, and a close-to-reality industrial casting example under varying boundary conditions are investigated as the example problems. We investigate the potential of several neural operator backbones, including Fourier neural operators (FNOs), deep operator networks (DeepONets), and a newly proposed implicit finite operator learning (iFOL) approach based on conditional neural fields. The results demonstrate that FNOs yield highly accurate solution operators on regular domains, where the global topology can be efficiently learned in the spectral domain, and iFOL offers efficient parametric operator learning capabilities for complex and irregular geometries. Furthermore, studies on training strategies, network decomposition, and training sample quality reveal that a monolithic training strategy using a single network is sufficient for accurate predictions, while training sample quality strongly influences performance. Overall, the present approach highlights the potential of physics-informed operator learning with a finite element-based loss as a unified and scalable approach for coupled multiphysics simulations.

AI.tldr: 提出基于有限元引导的物理信息算子学习框架，用于任意域上耦合PDE的多物理场问题，实现离散无关的预测，无需标记仿真数据。
AI.motivation: 解决多物理场问题中耦合PDE在任意域上的求解挑战，传统方法需要大量计算资源，而数据驱动方法需要大量标记数据。需要开发一种既能利用物理约束，又能实现高效预测的框架。
AI.method: 基于有限元加权残差公式的物理信息算子学习框架，使用JAX平台Folax实现。比较了FNO、DeepONet和新提出的iFOL（基于条件神经场的隐式有限算子学习）等神经网络算子骨干。采用整体训练策略，单个网络即可准确预测。
AI.result: 在非线性热力学问题上验证：FNO在规则域上精度高，能有效学习谱域全局拓扑；iFOL在复杂不规则几何上提供高效的参数化算子学习能力。训练样本质量对性能影响显著，整体训练策略足够准确。
AI.conclusion: 基于有限元损失的物理信息算子学习框架为耦合多物理场模拟提供了统一且可扩展的方法，展示了在复杂工程问题中的应用潜力。

title: Autoregressive Synthesis of Sparse and Semi-Structured Mixed-Type Data
summary: Synthetic data generation is a critical capability for data sharing, privacy compliance, system benchmarking and test data provisioning. Existing methods assume dense, fixed-schema tabular data, yet this assumption is increasingly at odds with modern data systems - from document databases, REST APIs to data lakes - which store and exchange data in sparse, semi-structured formats like JSON. Applying existing tabular methods to such data requires flattening of nested data into wide, sparse tables which scales poorly. We present Origami, an autoregressive transformer-based architecture that tokenizes data records, including nested objects and variable length arrays, into sequences of key, value and structural tokens. This representation natively handles sparsity, mixed types and hierarchical structure without flattening or imputation. Origami outperforms baselines spanning GAN, VAE, diffusion and autoregressive architectures on fidelity, utility and detection metrics across nearly all settings, while maintaining high privacy scores. On semi-structured datasets with up to 38% sparsity, baseline synthesizers either fail to scale or degrade substantially, while Origami maintains high-fidelity synthesis that is harder to distinguish from real data. To the best of our knowledge, Origami is the first architecture capable of natively modeling and generating semi-structured data end-to-end.

AI.tldr: Origami是一种基于自回归Transformer的架构，能够原生处理稀疏、半结构化数据（如JSON），无需扁平化或插补，在保真度、实用性和隐私保护方面优于现有方法。
AI.motivation: 现代数据系统（如文档数据库、REST API和数据湖）越来越多地使用稀疏、半结构化格式（如JSON）存储和交换数据。现有合成数据生成方法假设数据是密集、固定模式的表格数据，需要将嵌套数据扁平化为宽而稀疏的表格，这种方法扩展性差。
AI.method: Origami采用自回归Transformer架构，将数据记录（包括嵌套对象和可变长度数组）标记化为键、值和结构标记的序列。这种表示方法原生处理稀疏性、混合类型和层次结构，无需扁平化或插补。
AI.result: Origami在保真度、实用性和检测指标上优于GAN、VAE、扩散和自回归架构的基线方法，同时保持高隐私分数。在稀疏度高达38%的半结构化数据集上，基线合成器要么无法扩展，要么性能显著下降，而Origami保持高保真度合成，更难与真实数据区分。
AI.conclusion: Origami是第一个能够原生建模和生成端到端半结构化数据的架构，解决了现代数据系统中稀疏、半结构化数据合成的重要挑战。

title: GAC: Stabilizing Asynchronous RL Training for LLMs via Gradient Alignment Control
summary: Asynchronous execution is essential for scaling reinforcement learning (RL) to modern large model workloads, including large language models and AI agents, but it can fundamentally alter RL optimization behavior. While prior work on asynchronous RL focuses on training throughput and distributional correction, we show that naively applying asynchrony to policy-gradient updates can induce qualitatively different training dynamics and lead to severe training instability. Through systematic empirical and theoretical analysis, we identify a key signature of this instability: asynchronous training exhibits persistently high cosine similarity between consecutive policy gradients, in contrast to the near-orthogonal updates observed under synchronized training. This stale-aligned gradient effect amplifies correlated updates and increases the risk of overshooting and divergence. Motivated by this observation, we propose GRADIENT ALIGNMENT CONTROL(GAC), a simple dynamics-aware stabilization method that regulates asynchronous RL progress along stale-aligned directions via gradient projection. We establish convergence guarantees under bounded staleness and demonstrate empirically that GAC recovers stable, on-policy training dynamics and matches synchronized baselines even at high staleness.

AI.tldr: 异步强化学习中的梯度对齐问题：异步执行会改变策略梯度更新的动态，导致训练不稳定。作者提出梯度对齐控制(GAC)方法，通过梯度投影调节异步更新方向，恢复稳定训练。
AI.motivation: 现代大规模模型工作负载（如大语言模型和AI智能体）需要异步执行来扩展强化学习，但异步会从根本上改变RL优化行为。现有异步RL研究主要关注训练吞吐量和分布校正，而作者发现异步策略梯度更新会导致训练动态发生质变，引发严重的不稳定性。
AI.method: 提出梯度对齐控制(GAC)方法：通过系统实证和理论分析，识别异步训练不稳定的关键特征——连续策略梯度间持续保持高余弦相似度（与同步训练中的近正交更新形成对比）。GAC通过梯度投影调节异步RL在陈旧对齐方向上的进展，从而稳定训练动态。
AI.result: 理论分析：在有界陈旧性条件下建立了收敛保证。实证结果：GAC能够恢复稳定的、类似同策略的训练动态，即使在高陈旧性条件下也能匹配同步基线的性能。
AI.conclusion: 异步RL中的梯度对齐问题是训练不稳定的关键原因。提出的GAC方法简单有效，能够控制异步更新方向，确保稳定收敛，为大规模RL应用提供了可靠的异步训练解决方案。

title: TopoCurate:Modeling Interaction Topology for Tool-Use Agent Training
summary: Training tool-use agents typically relies on outcome-based filtering: Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) on successful trajectories and Reinforcement Learning (RL) on pass-rate-selected tasks. However, this paradigm ignores interaction dynamics: successful trajectories may lack error recovery or exhibit redundancy, while pass rates fail to distinguish structurally informative tasks from trivial ones. We propose \textbf{TopoCurate}, an interaction-aware framework that projects multi-trial rollouts from the same task into a unified semantic quotient topology. By merging equivalent action-observation states, this projection transforms scattered linear trajectories into a structured manifold that explicitly captures how tool invocations and environmental responses drive the divergence between effective strategies and failure modes. Leveraging this representation, we introduce a dual-selection mechanism: for SFT, we prioritize trajectories demonstrating reflective recovery, semantic efficiency, and strategic diversity to mitigate covariate shift and mode collapse; for RL, we select tasks with high error branch ratios and strategic heterogeneity, maximizing gradient Signal-to-Noise Ratio to address vanishing signals in sparse-reward settings. Evaluations on BFCLv3 and Tau2 Bench show that TopoCurate achieves consistent gains of 4.2\% (SFT) and 6.9\% (RL) over state-of-the-art baselines. We will release the code and data soon for further investigations.

AI.tldr: TopoCurate：一个基于交互感知的工具使用智能体训练框架，通过将多轮试验轨迹投影到统一的语义商拓扑中，实现双选择机制优化SFT和RL训练，在基准测试中显著超越现有方法。
AI.motivation: 当前基于结果过滤的训练方法（SFT和RL）存在局限性：成功轨迹可能缺乏错误恢复能力或存在冗余，而通过率无法区分结构信息丰富的任务和简单任务。这些方法忽略了交互动态，导致训练效率低下。
AI.method: 提出TopoCurate框架：1）将同一任务的多轮试验轨迹投影到统一的语义商拓扑中，合并等效的动作-观察状态；2）引入双选择机制：对于SFT，优先选择具有反思恢复、语义效率和策略多样性的轨迹；对于RL，选择具有高错误分支比和策略异质性的任务。
AI.result: 在BFCLv3和Tau2 Bench基准测试中，TopoCurate相比最先进基线方法，SFT获得4.2%的稳定提升，RL获得6.9%的提升。该方法有效解决了稀疏奖励设置中的梯度信号消失问题。
AI.conclusion: TopoCurate通过交互感知的拓扑表示和双选择机制，显著提升了工具使用智能体的训练效果，为理解策略有效性和失败模式之间的动态关系提供了新视角。

title: Efficient RLVR Training via Weighted Mutual Information Data Selection
summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) plays a central role in improving the reasoning and alignment of large language models, yet its efficiency critically depends on how training data are selected. Existing online selection strategies predominantly rely on difficulty-based heuristics, favouring datapoints with intermediate success rates, implicitly equating difficulty with informativeness and neglecting epistemic uncertainty arising from limited evidence. We introduce InSight, an INformation-guided data SamplInG metHod for RL Training, grounded in a weighted mutual information objective. By modeling data outcomes with Bayesian latent success rates, we show that expected uncertainty reduction decomposes into complementary difficulty- and evidence-dependent components, revealing a fundamental limitation of difficulty-only selection. Leveraging this observation, InSight constructs a stable acquisition score based on the mean belief of datapoints' success rather than noisy sampled outcomes, and naturally extends to multi-rollout settings common in reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR). Extensive experiments demonstrate that InSight consistently achieves state-of-the-art performance and improves training efficiency, including a +1.41 average gain on Planning & Mathmatics benchmarks, +1.01 improvement on general reasoning, and up to ~2.2x acceleration, with negligible additional computational overhead.

AI.tldr: InSight：基于信息理论的数据采样方法，通过建模贝叶斯潜在成功率，结合难度和证据不确定性，提升RL训练效率
AI.motivation: 现有RL训练数据选择策略主要依赖基于难度的启发式方法，假设难度等于信息量，忽略了有限证据带来的认知不确定性，导致训练效率受限
AI.method: 提出InSight方法，基于加权互信息目标，通过贝叶斯潜在成功率建模数据结果，构建稳定的获取分数（基于平均信念而非噪声采样结果），可扩展到多轮次RLVR设置
AI.result: 在Planning & Mathematics基准上平均提升+1.41，通用推理提升+1.01，训练加速最高达~2.2倍，计算开销可忽略
AI.conclusion: InSight通过结合难度和证据不确定性，克服了纯难度选择的局限性，实现了SOTA性能和训练效率提升，为RL训练数据选择提供了更理论基础的方法

title: Scalable Multi-Task Low-Rank Model Adaptation
comment: Published as a conference paper at ICLR 2026. 21 pages, 4 figures, 11 tables. Code is available
summary: Scaling multi-task low-rank adaptation (LoRA) to a large number of tasks induces catastrophic performance degradation, such as an accuracy drop from 88.2% to 2.0% on DOTA when scaling from 5 to 15 tasks. This failure is due to parameter and representation misalignment. We find that existing solutions, like regularization and dynamic routing, fail at scale because they are constrained by a fundamental trade-off: strengthening regularization to reduce inter-task conflict inadvertently suppresses the essential feature discrimination required for effective routing. In this work, we identify two root causes for this trade-off. First, uniform regularization disrupts inter-task knowledge sharing: shared underlying knowledge concentrates in high-SV components (89% alignment on Flanv2->BBH). Uniform regularization forces high-SV components to update in orthogonal directions, directly disrupting the shared knowledge. Second, Conflict Amplification: Applying LoRA at the component-level (e.g., W_q, W_v) amplifies gradient conflicts; we show block-level adaptation reduces this conflict by 76% with only 50% parameters. Based on these insights, we propose mtLoRA, a scalable solution with three novel designs: 1) Spectral-Aware Regularization to selectively orthogonalize low-SV components while preserving high-SV shared knowledge, 2) Block-Level Adaptation to mitigate conflict amplification and largely improve parameter efficiency, and 3) Fine-Grained Routing using dimension-specific weights for superior expressive power. On four large-scale (15-25 tasks) vision (DOTA and iNat2018) and NLP (Dolly-15k and BBH) benchmarks, mtLoRA achieves 91.7%, 81.5%, 44.5% and 38.5% accuracy on DOTA, iNat2018, Dolly-15k and BBH respectively, outperforming the state-of-the-art by 2.3% on average while using 47% fewer parameters and 24% less training time.

AI.tldr: mtLoRA通过谱感知正则化、块级适配和细粒度路由解决多任务LoRA扩展时的性能退化问题，在多个基准上实现SOTA性能，同时减少参数和训练时间。
AI.motivation: 多任务低秩适配（LoRA）扩展到大量任务时会导致灾难性性能下降（如从5个任务扩展到15个任务时，DOTA准确率从88.2%降至2.0%），现有解决方案受限于正则化与特征判别之间的基本权衡。
AI.method: 提出mtLoRA，包含三个创新设计：1）谱感知正则化，选择性正交化低奇异值分量，同时保留高奇异值共享知识；2）块级适配，减少梯度冲突并提高参数效率；3）细粒度路由，使用维度特定权重增强表达能力。
AI.result: 在四个大规模（15-25任务）视觉（DOTA和iNat2018）和NLP（Dolly-15k和BBH）基准测试中，mtLoRA分别达到91.7%、81.5%、44.5%和38.5%的准确率，平均优于SOTA 2.3%，同时使用参数减少47%，训练时间减少24%。
AI.conclusion: mtLoRA通过解决多任务LoRA扩展的根本问题，实现了可扩展的多任务适配，在性能、参数效率和训练效率方面均显著优于现有方法。

title: Semantic Similarity is a Spurious Measure of Comic Understanding: Lessons Learned from Hallucinations in a Benchmarking Experiment
comment: 8 pages, 2 figures, 3 tables. Includes link to code
summary: A system that enables blind or visually impaired users to access comics/manga would introduce a new medium of storytelling to this community. However, no such system currently exists. Generative vision-language models (VLMs) have shown promise in describing images and understanding comics, but most research on comic understanding is limited to panel-level analysis. To fully support blind and visually impaired users, greater attention must be paid to page-level understanding and interpretation. In this work, we present a preliminary benchmark of VLM performance on comic interpretation tasks. We identify and categorize hallucinations that emerge during this process, organizing them into generalized object-hallucination taxonomies. We conclude with guidance on future research, emphasizing hallucination mitigation and improved data curation for comic interpretation.

AI.tldr: 该论文研究了如何利用视觉语言模型帮助视障人士理解漫画，建立了漫画理解的基准测试，并分析了模型在解释过程中产生的幻觉问题。
AI.motivation: 目前没有系统能帮助盲人或视障用户访问漫画/漫画这一叙事媒介。虽然生成式视觉语言模型在图像描述和漫画理解方面显示出潜力，但现有研究大多局限于面板级分析，需要更关注页面级理解和解释来全面支持视障用户。
AI.method: 提出了一个视觉语言模型在漫画解释任务上的初步基准测试，识别并分类了在此过程中出现的幻觉现象，将其组织成通用的对象幻觉分类法。
AI.result: 建立了漫画解释的基准测试框架，系统性地识别和分类了视觉语言模型在漫画理解中产生的各种幻觉问题。
AI.conclusion: 未来研究应重点关注幻觉缓解和改进漫画解释的数据策展，以更好地支持视障用户访问漫画内容。

title: LFPO: Likelihood-Free Policy Optimization for Masked Diffusion Models
summary: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has achieved remarkable success in improving autoregressive models, especially in domains requiring correctness like mathematical reasoning and code generation. However, directly applying such paradigms to Diffusion Large Language Models (dLLMs) is fundamentally hindered by the intractability of exact likelihood computation, which forces existing methods to rely on high-variance approximations. To bridge this gap, we propose Likelihood-Free Policy Optimization (LFPO), a native framework that maps the concept of vector field flow matching to the discrete token space. Specifically, LFPO formulates alignment as geometric velocity rectification, which directly optimizes denoising logits via contrastive updates. This design effectively bypasses the errors inherent in likelihood approximation, yielding the precise gradient estimation. Furthermore, LFPO enforce consistency by predicting final solutions from intermediate steps, effectively straightening the probability flow to enable high-quality generation with significantly fewer iterations. Extensive experiments demonstrate that LFPO not only outperforms state-of-the-art baselines on code and reasoning benchmarks but also accelerates inference by approximately 20% through reduced diffusion steps.

AI.tldr: LFPO是一种针对扩散大语言模型的免似然策略优化框架，通过几何速度校正直接优化去噪logits，避免了传统RLVR方法中的似然计算难题，在代码生成和推理任务上表现优异且能加速推理20%。
AI.motivation: 传统的可验证奖励强化学习（RLVR）在自回归模型中取得了显著成功，但直接应用于扩散大语言模型（dLLMs）存在根本性障碍，因为精确似然计算不可行，现有方法只能依赖高方差近似，导致效果受限。
AI.method: LFPO将向量场流匹配概念映射到离散标记空间，将对齐问题表述为几何速度校正，通过对比更新直接优化去噪logits，绕过似然近似误差；同时通过从中间步骤预测最终解来强制执行一致性，拉直概率流以实现高质量生成。
AI.result: LFPO在代码和推理基准测试中超越了现有最先进基线，同时通过减少扩散步骤将推理速度提升了约20%。
AI.conclusion: LFPO为扩散大语言模型提供了一种免似然的策略优化框架，有效解决了传统RLVR方法在dLLMs中应用的似然计算难题，在保持高质量生成的同时显著提升了推理效率。

title: Rate-Distortion Signatures of Generalization and Information Trade-offs
summary: Generalization to novel visual conditions remains a central challenge for both human and machine vision, yet standard robustness metrics offer limited insight into how systems trade accuracy for robustness. We introduce a rate-distortion-theoretic framework that treats stimulus-response behavior as an effective communication channel, derives rate-distortion (RD) frontiers from confusion matrices, and summarizes each system with two interpretable geometric signatures - slope ($β$) and curvature ($κ$) - which capture the marginal cost and abruptness of accuracy-robustness trade-offs. Applying this framework to human psychophysics and 18 deep vision models under controlled image perturbations, we compare generalization geometry across model architectures and training regimes. We find that both biological and artificial systems follow a common lossy-compression principle but occupy systematically different regions of RD space. In particular, humans exhibit smoother, more flexible trade-offs, whereas modern deep networks operate in steeper and more brittle regimes even at matched accuracy. Across training regimes, robustness training induces systematic but dissociable shifts in beta/kappa, revealing cases where improved robustness or accuracy does not translate into more human-like generalization geometry. These results demonstrate that RD geometry provides a compact, model-agnostic lens for comparing generalization behavior across systems beyond standard accuracy-based metrics.

AI.tldr: 论文提出基于率失真理论的框架，用斜率β和曲率κ两个几何特征量化视觉系统在准确性与鲁棒性之间的权衡，应用于人类心理物理学和18个深度视觉模型，发现两者遵循共同的有损压缩原理但占据不同的率失真空间区域。
AI.motivation: 标准鲁棒性指标对系统如何在准确性和鲁棒性之间进行权衡提供有限洞察，需要一种能够量化比较不同视觉系统泛化行为的模型无关框架。
AI.method: 引入率失真理论框架，将刺激-响应行为视为有效通信信道，从混淆矩阵推导率失真前沿，用斜率β（边际成本）和曲率κ（权衡突变性）两个几何特征总结系统特性，应用于人类心理物理学数据和18个深度视觉模型。
AI.result: 生物和人工系统都遵循有损压缩原理，但占据不同的率失真空间：人类表现出更平滑、更灵活的权衡，而现代深度网络即使在匹配准确度下也处于更陡峭、更脆弱的区域；鲁棒性训练引起β/κ的系统性但可分离的变化。
AI.conclusion: 率失真几何为比较系统间泛化行为提供了紧凑、模型无关的视角，超越了基于准确度的标准指标，揭示了人类与AI视觉系统在泛化几何上的系统性差异。

title: Learning from Synthetic Data Improves Multi-hop Reasoning
